---
pagetitle: "DECK# — Layered Card Annotation System"
description: "An open-source standardized annotation system for regular playing cards"
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    toc: false
format-links: false
---

::: {.text-center .py-5}
![](docs/assets/logo.svg){width="120px" fig-alt="DECK# Logo" fig-align="center"}

# [DECK#]{style="color: var(--bs-body-color); font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 4rem;"}

**A layered annotation protocol for standard playing card decks**
*Un protocollo di annotazione stratificata per mazzi di carte standard*

`version 0.7.0 · draft · CC BY 4.0`
:::

---

::: {.grid .gap-4 .py-4}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .p-4 .h-100 .shadow-sm}
### English

A standardized open-source system for annotating a standard 52+2 deck with two permanent markers, enabling complex board games from materials available anywhere.

[Specification](docs/en/decksharp_specs.html){.btn .btn-primary .mt-2 .me-2}
[Annotation Guide](docs/en/decksharp_annotation_guide.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2 .me-2}
[Design Guide](docs/en/decksharp_design_guide.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2 .me-2}
[Design Notes](docs/en/decksharp_notes.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .p-4 .h-100 .shadow-sm}
### Italiano

Un sistema open source standardizzato per annotare un mazzo standard 52+2 con due pennarelli permanenti, per costruire giochi da tavolo complessi con materiali reperibili ovunque.

[Specifiche](docs/it/decksharp_specs.html){.btn .btn-primary .mt-2 .me-2}
[Guida all'Annotazione](docs/it/decksharp_guida_annotazione.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2 .me-2}
[Guida al Design](docs/it/decksharp_guida_design.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2 .me-2}
[Note di Design](docs/it/decksharp_notes.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .mt-2}
:::
:::

:::

---

## What is DECK#?

DECK# is a layered annotation protocol for a standard playing card deck (52 + 2 Jokers). Its purpose is to allow anyone to build and play complex board games using materials available anywhere, at minimal cost: a standard French-suited deck and two permanent markers.

Inspired by **Piecepack** (James Kyle, 2000), DECK# is an open system that defines a shared graphic vocabulary — 7 geometric glyphs, spatial zones, and connectivity markers — without prescribing game mechanics or semantics. Games built on DECK# assign their own meanings to this vocabulary.

DECK# is **language-agnostic**: the entire graphic vocabulary consists of geometric glyphs and Arabic numerals. No text is necessary or permitted in annotations.

::: {.grid .gap-3 .mt-3}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .p-3 .shadow-sm}
**7 primary glyphs**
`—  •  +  ×  △  ○  □`
Fixed vocabulary, open semantics.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .p-3 .shadow-sm}
**2 markers only**
Black and red permanent ink.
Color is derived, never a free choice.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .p-3 .shadow-sm}
**Two profiles**
Base (hand-held cards) and
Extended (grid/tactical tiles).
:::
:::

:::
